content.full_text:
August 25, 1992


Jane Granzer
300-G Avenue
Coronado, CA  92118

                                    Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                         Our File No. I-92-422

Dear Ms. Granzer:

     You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹  You have not
identified a particular candidate or committee on whose behalf you
are requesting this advice.  Therefore, we treat your letter as a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).²

                              QUESTION

     What campaign disclosure forms are required to be filed by
candidates who establish a jointly controlled committee.

                              CONCLUSION

     Before soliciting or raising funds, each candidate must file
a Candidate Intention Statement (Form 501).  In addition, a
Campaign Bank Account Statement (Form 502) must be filed within 10
days of opening a bank account.  If the candidates receive
contributions of $1,000 or more in connection with their election,
they must form a committee and file a Statement of Organization
(Form 410) within 10 days of receiving $1,000.  Candidates with a
controlled committee disclose contributions received and
expenditures made on an Officeholder, Candidate, and Controlled
Committee Campaign Statement--Long Form (Form 490).  Loan activity

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95812  •  (916) 322-5660

Jane Granzer
I-92-422
Page 2


is reported by the committee on Schedule B of the Form 490 as
described in the analysis below.

FACTS

You are the treasurer for three candidates who wish to form a
joint committee for their election to city office. You have
stated that there are no local limitations on contributions for
these candidates and that the election is not a special election.

ANALYSIS

Section 85200 requires candidates to file a Candidate Inten-
tion Statement (Form 501) prior to soliciting or accepting any
contributions. In addition, a campaign bank account must be
established at a financial institution located in California.
Within 10 days of establishing the campaign bank account, the
candidate must file a Campaign Bank Account Statement (Form 502)
with the Commission. (Section 85201(b).)

Section 82013(a) defines "committee" as any person or
combination of persons who directly or indirectly receives
contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year. Section
84101(a) requires every committee qualified under section 82013(a)
to file a Statement of Organization (Form 410) with the Secretary
of State within 10 days after receiving contributions totaling
$1,000 or more in a calendar year. In addition to filing the
original and one copy of Form 410 with the Secretary of State, the
committee must file a copy with the local filing officer with whom
it will file the originals of its campaign reports.

Committees controlled by candidates are required to file
periodic campaign reports (Form 490) disclosing financial activity
during the period covered by the report. (Campaign Disclosure
Information Manual A, page 18.)

According to the facts presented in your letter and in our
telephone conversations, it appears that the activities of the
candidates would require the following:

*    Each candidate must file a Form 501 and Form 502. The bank
     account number on the Form 502 will be the same for all three
     candidates.

*    The committee must file a Form 410 signed by each candidate
     and the treasurer.

*    The committee must file a complete Form 490 attaching three
     cover pages--one cover page for each candidate's signature.

*    Each candidate must file a Form 490 consisting of a cover
     page, allocation page (if applicable), and a summary page.
     The cover page will contain appropriate information in item
     II referencing the name of the committee.

Jane Granzer
I-92-422
Page 3


Form 501 does not require the disclosure of loans.  Loans
received are reported on Schedule B, Part I of the Form 490.
Loans that are repaid or forgiven are shown on Schedule B, Part II
of the Form 490 (refer to Information Manual A, page 27.)

Approximately 10 days after receiving the Statement of
Organization (Form 410), the Secretary of State will issue the
committee an identification number.  This number is required on
campaign statements filed under the Act.  If you need information
concerning a tax identification number (for example, if your bank
requires an identification number to open an account) you will
need to contact the Franchise Tax Board at 1-800-338-0505 or the
Internal Revenue Service at 1-800-829-1040.

If you have additional questions concerning these matters,
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Assistant Chief Counsel

[signature]

By:  Sandra L. Taylor
     Political Reform Consultant
